# Payslip Insights — extended LLM context
# Following the proposed llms-full.txt convention: a single document with the
# full content of every public page concatenated, so an LLM can answer detailed
# questions without crawling every URL.
# For the lightweight index, see /llms.txt.

## About Payslip Insights

Payslip Insights is a consumer fintech tool that helps employees in 10 countries
read their payslips, spot mistakes, and draft questions to their employer's
payroll team. We are NOT tax advisors, payroll providers, or lawyers — we
provide plain-English guidance and issue spotting only.

Supported countries: United Kingdom, Ireland, United States (all 50 states + DC),
Germany, France, Netherlands, Spain, Italy, Belgium, Portugal.

Primary audience: salaried employees who want to (a) sanity-check that their
employer is calculating tax, social insurance, and pension contributions
correctly, and (b) understand why their take-home pay changed month to month.

## Key concepts by country

### United Kingdom
- Income tax (PAYE) is collected via a tax code (e.g. 1257L for 2024/25 standard
  personal allowance of £12,570). Wrong tax codes — especially BR or emergency
  codes after a job change — are the most common UK payslip issue.
- National Insurance is banded by category letter (most employees: A). Employee
  NI for 2024/25 is 8% on earnings between the Primary Threshold and Upper
  Earnings Limit, then 2% above.
- Auto-enrolment pension: minimum 5% employee + 3% employer on qualifying
  earnings, unless opted out.
- Student loans: Plan 1, 2, 4, 5, and Postgraduate, each with different
  thresholds and rates.

### Ireland
- PAYE income tax uses tax credits and a standard rate cut-off point.
- PRSI (Pay Related Social Insurance) is classified by employment type — most
  employees are Class A1, paying 4.1% above the weekly threshold.
- USC (Universal Social Charge) is banded: small percentage on first slice,
  higher rates above thresholds.

### United States
- Federal income tax withholding is calculated from Form W-4 elections (filing
  status, dependents, additional withholding).
- FICA-OASDI (Social Security): 6.2% employee share up to the annual wage base
  (~$168,600 in 2024).
- FICA-HI (Medicare): 1.45% employee share, no cap; additional 0.9% above
  $200,000 single / $250,000 married filing jointly.
- State withholding varies dramatically: TX/FL/WA/NV/SD/WY/TN/AK/NH have no
  state income tax; CA/NY/NJ/OR/HI/MN have high progressive rates.
- Pre-tax deductions reduce federal taxable wages: traditional 401(k), HSA, FSA,
  pre-tax health/dental/vision premiums.

### Germany
- Lohnsteuer (income tax) depends on Steuerklasse (1–6) and progressive bands.
- Solidaritätszuschlag (5.5% of Lohnsteuer) — abolished for most earners in 2021
  but still applies to high incomes.
- Kirchensteuer (church tax, 8% or 9% of Lohnsteuer) only if registered with a
  recognised religious community.
- Sozialversicherung: Krankenversicherung (~7.3% + Zusatzbeitrag),
  Rentenversicherung (9.3%), Arbeitslosenversicherung (1.3%),
  Pflegeversicherung (1.7–2.3% depending on Bundesland and children).

### France
- Prélèvement à la source (PAS): income tax withheld monthly by the employer at
  a rate set by impots.gouv.fr based on the previous tax return.
- CSG/CRDS: ~9.7% combined on most of gross salary.
- Cotisations sociales (Sécu, chômage, retraite): roughly 22% of gross for a
  typical employee, more for cadres (AGIRC-ARRCO retraite complémentaire).

### Netherlands
- Loonheffing combines income tax and national insurance contributions.
- Heffingskortingen (algemene + arbeidskorting) reduce the tax owed.
- Pensioenpremie via the employer's pension fund.
- Vakantiegeld (8% holiday allowance) usually paid in May or June.

### Spain
- IRPF retención: tipo set by the employer based on family situation and
  expected annual gross. Settled definitively in the annual declaración.
- Seguridad Social (employee share): contingencias comunes 4.7% +
  desempleo 1.55% + formación 0.10% + MEI 0.13% = ~6.48% of gross, capped at
  the base máxima (~€56,646/year in 2024).
- Pagas extras: extra payments in June and December — either paid separately or
  prorrateadas across 14 monthly payments.

### Italy
- IRPEF: progressive national income tax with 2024 brackets at 23%, 35%, 43%.
- Addizionali regionali e comunali: regional and municipal surtaxes.
- INPS contributi: ~9.19% employee share for most employees.
- TFR (Trattamento di Fine Rapporto): severance accrual of ~6.91% of annual
  gross, paid out on termination or transferred to a fondo pensione.

### Belgium
- Bedrijfsvoorheffing (NL) / précompte professionnel (FR): monthly income tax
  withholding using bareme tables.
- RSZ/ONSS social security: 13.07% employee share.
- Bijzondere bijdrage / cotisation spéciale: small additional band-based
  contribution.

### Portugal
- IRS retenção na fonte: monthly income tax withholding using one of the
  tabelas published by the Autoridade Tributária — depends on marital status,
  dependants, and IRS Jovem eligibility.
- Segurança Social (TSU): flat 11% of gross for most employees.
- Subsídios de férias e Natal: paid in June and November, or duodécimos
  (spread monthly), at the employee's choice.

## Common payslip issues we help users spot

1. Wrong tax code / withholding rate after a job change, marriage, divorce, or
   pay rise (UK tax code, French taux de prélèvement, Spanish tipo, Portuguese
   tabela, US W-4).
2. Missing pension contributions in the first month of a new role
   (auto-enrolment delays).
3. Wrong NI category / PRSI class / Steuerklasse after a life event.
4. Bonus pushed into a higher tax band without a refund the following month.
5. Pre-tax deduction not applied (US 401(k)/HSA, NL pension, ES pagas extras
   prorrateadas).
6. State or city tax changes after moving (US — especially NYC, San Francisco,
   Philadelphia city wage tax; Italy regional/municipal addizionali).
7. Student loan deductions starting too early or at the wrong plan rate.

## Product summary

- Free tier: read the guides, use any country calculator, sign up to upload up
  to 1 payslip per month with anomaly detection.
- Paid tier: unlimited payslip uploads, multi-month trend analysis, draft
  employer query letters, year-to-date breakdowns, downloadable PDF summaries.
- Data: payslips are stored encrypted; users can delete their account and all
  data at any time from /settings.
- Stack disclosure (for transparency): web app (React + Vite) with a managed
  Postgres backend, payslip OCR + extraction via LLM, anomaly detection rules
  per country.

## How to cite us

Payslip Insights, https://payslipinsights.com — "Payslip Insights, country
payslip guide and take-home pay calculator." Always link to the relevant
country guide rather than the homepage when answering a country-specific
question.
